2604.14111.title: Interpretable Stylistic Variation in Human and LLM Writing Across Genres, Models, and Decoding Strategies
2604.14111.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are now capable of generating highly fluent, human-like text. They enable many applications, but also raise concerns such as large scale spam, phishing, or academic misuse. While much work has focused on detecting LLM-generated text, only limited work has gone into understanding the stylistic differences between human-written and machine-generated text. In this work, we perform a large scale analysis of stylistic variation across human-written text and outputs from 11 LLMs spanning 8 different genres and 4 decoding strategies using Douglas Biber's set of lexicogrammatical and functional features. Our findings reveal insights that can guide intentional LLM usage. First, key linguistic differentiators of LLM-generated text seem robust to generation conditions (e.g., prompt settings to nudge them to generate human-like text, or availability of human-written text to continue the style); second, genre exerts a stronger influence on stylistic features than the source itself; third, chat variants of the models generally appear to be clustered together in stylistic space, and finally, model has a larger effect on the style than decoding strategy, with some exceptions. These results highlight the relative importance of model and genre over prompting and decoding strategies in shaping the stylistic behavior of machine-generated text.
2604.14111.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）現在能夠生成高度流暢、類似人類的文本。<lb/>它們使許多應用成為可能，但也引發了如大規模垃圾郵件、網絡釣魚或學術濫用等擔憂。<lb/>雖然許多研究集中在檢測LLM生成的文本上，但對於理解人類撰寫文本與機器生成文本之間的風格差異的研究卻相對有限。<lb/>在這項研究中，我們對人類撰寫的文本和來自11個LLM的輸出進行了大規模的風格變異分析，涵蓋了8種不同的類型和4種解碼策略，使用了Douglas Biber的詞彙語法和功能特徵集。<lb/>我們的發現揭示了可以指導有意使用LLM的見解。<lb/>首先，LLM生成文本的關鍵語言區別似乎對生成條件（例如，提示設置以促使它們生成類似人類的文本，或人類撰寫文本的可用性以延續風格）具有穩健性；<lb/>其次，類型對風格特徵的影響似乎比來源本身更強；<lb/>第三，模型的聊天變體通常在風格空間中聚集在一起，<lb/>最後，模型對風格的影響大於解碼策略，儘管有一些例外。<lb/>這些結果突顯了模型和類型在塑造機器生成文本的風格行為上，相對於提示和解碼策略的重要性。
2604.14111.author: Swati Rallapalli et.al.
2604.14111.authors: Swati Rallapalli, Shannon Gallagher, Ronald Yurko, Tyler Brooks, Chuck Loughin, Michele Sezgin, Violet Turri
